originalAlarm.description: Memory dynamically allocated by `malloc`, indirectly via call to `inet_ehash_locks_alloc()` on line 43 is not freed after the last access at line 46, column 1.

reasoning: Line 37 has '// ERROR' — known VBDB bug. Memory leak in 218ad12f42e.c: when NUMA=T, inet_ehash_locks_alloc() allocates x. In inet_ehash_locks_free() with NUMA=T, the free(x) at L32 is only executed when size > PAGE_SIZE. When size <= PAGE_SIZE (statically possible path), x is set to NULL at L37 without being freed — memory leak. The bug manifests only when NUMA=T (NUMA=F uses the unconditional free at L35). PC = NUMA=T exactly matches. Classification: TRUE.
